meta.tagline: Every VC tool asks who has already emitted a signal. We ask what evidence should exist if the claim were true, search for exactly that, and price the gap.
meta.challenge: Hack-Nation 6th Global AI Hackathon — Challenge 02, The VC Brain (Maschmeyer Group)
meta.hand_authored_statement: This file was written by hand before the worker existed, so that every view could render end-to-end at hour 6 with no backend. worker/export_demo.py replaces these values block by block as the ledger fills, and records which blocks it replaced.
meta.scope_statement: Sourcing → Screening → Diligence → Decision. Everything downstream of Decision — portfolio monitoring, follow-on, fund ops, exit — is out of scope and has no nav item.
meta.chokepoint: worker/ledger.py :: read_observations(asof) — the only function in the codebase that reads the observation table, and it always filters WHERE observed_at <= :asof.
meta.no_averaged_axes: There is no averaged axis anywhere in this system. Not in the schema, not in this file, not on screen.

provenance_of_this_file.statement: Every block not listed as derived came from the hand-authored overrides file, which is committed and readable. We would rather show you which numbers we wrote by hand than let you guess.

thesis.name: Maschmeyer Group — early-stage B2B infrastructure
thesis.configurable_note: Six persisted fields. Nothing here is hardcoded to one fund; editing any field re-ranks the board and can move an opportunity across the decision gate.
thesis.risk_appetite_is_load_bearing: Risk appetite maps to the maximum posterior interval width at which capital deploys. That makes the thesis load-bearing on our core mechanic rather than a filter bar: at risk_appetite=low, Ledgerline becomes probe_further and Northgate stays probe_further; at high, Northgate becomes conditional.

soft_weights.note: Soft weights order the board. They never combine the three axes into one number — the decision object reads the axes separately and names a binding axis.

thesis.conviction_threshold_note: An opportunity opens on its own when a person's founder-axis lower bound crosses 55.0 without any human input. That is what makes this an operating system rather than a dashboard.

memory.title: Memory — one append-only ledger
memory.plain_line: Nothing in here is ever updated or deleted. Every score is a pure function of the rows, which is why a score can never quietly reset.
memory.enrichment_rule: provenance_class='derived' rows carry derived_from links back to the observation they were computed from, and the ledger view renders the chain.

axis_writeback.title: Each axis feeds back into Memory to sharpen future scoring
axis_writeback.plain_line: When a Market verdict lands for a sector, it changes the starting point for the next company we see in that sector.

rows.note: Northgate was scored against the updated sector prior, not the original one. The row is in the ledger and dated.
Small, and we print how small. Two verdicts is not a prior worth much yet.

compound_query.title: Multi-attribute reasoning in one pass
compound_query.plain_line: One question, parsed once, answered once. Not a chatbot firing five filters in sequence — the parse is rendered so you can check it.
compound_query.query_text: technical founder, Berlin, AI infra, enterprise traction, no prior VC backing, top-tier accelerator
compound_query.query_source: The brief's own canonical example query, typed verbatim.

chips.note: We have two observations that speak to customer type across the matching set. This resolves as a probability with its n printed, not as a filter that would silently drop everyone we simply have no data on.

Deliberately NOT a hard filter. A hard filter on the absence of a signal deletes exactly the cold-start founders this product exists to find, because absence of evidence of funding and evidence of absence are not the same row.

chips.reason: Declined as a pedigree channel — admission to a top-tier accelerator IS the network gate this product exists to replace. See honesty.not_collected.

n_matching.basis: no source collected, so no count exists to report

compound_query.closing_line: A chip that resolves to a reasoned refusal is worth more than one that resolves to a number we could not defend.

funnel.title: Sourcing funnel — one arithmetic, computed from stage_transition rows
funnel.plain_line: How many people we found, how many we could actually reach, and how many reached a decision. The gap between the first two numbers is our largest structural weakness, so we print it rather than let you find it.
funnel.arithmetic: 63 discovered → 21 contactable → 9 outbound activated + 17 inbound = 26 entered screening → 14 screened out → 12 advanced past the screen → 3 reached a decision, 9 did not.
funnel.arithmetic_check: 9 outbound + 17 inbound = 26. 26 − 14 screened out = 12 advanced. 12 − 3 decided = 9 stalled. 6 + 5 + 2 + 1 = 14 screen-out reasons.

stalled_by_stage.note: Of the 12 that cleared the screen, 2 are still sitting at the screening stage blocked on a human reply and never entered diligence; 10 entered diligence, 3 of which reached a decision and 7 of which stalled there. 2 + 7 = 9.

reliability.plain_line: The brief asks how fast AND how reliably an opportunity moves from first signal to decision. Reliability is the second number, and it is not flattering.

timing.title: Time from first signal to decision — the quantity the brief actually names
timing.not_to_be_confused_with: honesty.latency, which measures compute and is four orders of magnitude smaller. Conflating them would be the easiest number in this system to get wrong.
timing.plain_line: Excluding the wait for a founder to reply: 41 minutes. Including it: 6.2 hours. The human wait is the real bottleneck and we would rather name it than hide it in an average.
timing.thin_cell_note: Three decisions is not a distribution. We print the median anyway with n=3 beside it, because a hidden number is worse than a thin one.

contactability.title: Discovered is not the same as reachable
contactability.plain_line: 42 of 63 people we surfaced have no contactable channel at all. That is the honest ceiling on an outbound product and it is our largest structural weakness.

screen.title: Screen — four named rules, and a screen-out is never a delete
screen.plain_line: We remove clearly non-viable submissions with a stated reason. The row stays in the ledger, so a founder we screened out today can re-enter later.
screen.reversibility_statement: A screen-out is a stage_transition row, never a delete. Our own anti-network-gate thesis applied to our own funnel: being turned down once is not a permanent state in this system, because nothing in this system is ever overwritten.

rules.text: No product artifact of any kind — no domain, no repo, no filing, no deck content describing a built thing.
Out of thesis on a hard filter — sector, stage or geography.
Excluded category — regulated verticals the fund does not enter.
No company identity in the deck — we cannot key observations to an entity, so nothing could be verified against anything.

examples.reason_shown: No product artifact of any kind. The deck describes an intention; there is no domain, repo, filing or shipped thing to observe. We have nothing to verify a claim against.
Out of thesis: consumer marketplace, Series A, US-only. Fails three hard filters. This is a thesis mismatch, not a judgement about the company.
No company identity anywhere in the submission. Without an entity key there is no observation to attach and no claim that could be checked. Resubmit with a name and we will screen it again.

screen.examples_note: Three of the fourteen are shown so that '14 screened out' has an observable referent rather than being a number with nothing behind it.
screen.over_collection_line: We ask for two fields now. If we need more, we ask one specific question, once, and tell you why.

portfolio.title: Portfolio conflict check
portfolio.plain_line: Before a decision, we check the existing book for a conflicting position. This is part of the decision, not portfolio monitoring, which is out of scope.
portfolio.out_of_scope_note: We hold four positions and check against them at decision time. We do not monitor them here — follow-on, fund ops, exit and LP reporting are explicitly out of scope and have no view in this product.

signal_feed.title: Signal feed
signal_feed.plain_line: The ranked board of everyone we surfaced this week. Flip the switch and the ranking is recomputed with access signals regressed out. Watch the order change.
signal_feed.n_rows_note: Twenty of sixty-three shown. The rest are in the ledger at the same asof.
signal_feed.headline_line: Every tool in this market ranks founders by how visible they are. Visibility is already priced. We rank on what is left. Forty-seven of these sixty-three have never met an investor.
signal_feed.neutralization_note: Access-neutralized. We regress out only signals we actually collect — company age, domain age, whether the trademark carried an attorney of record, and whether a funding announcement exists — plus one hand-coded field, prior employer VC-backed, n=20 hand-coded from public bios and labelled as such. We deliberately do not print an R²: it would not be externally anchored, and the reorder carries the argument on its own.

neutralization_inputs.not_used_reason: We declined those sources in our own not-collected ledger, so we cannot honestly residualize on them. Residualizing on data we refused to collect would be the first fabricated number in a product whose thesis is that fabrication is structurally impossible.

r_squared.basis: deliberately not computed — it would not be externally anchored

trigger.label: Trigger fired — no human input
trigger.headline: Auto-opened 3 opportunities this run with no human input. M. O. crossed the conviction threshold of 55.0 on a self-filed 1(b) trademark with no attorney of record, plus a live checkout endpoint on the matching domain.
trigger.note: The threshold is the thesis object's conviction_threshold, reused. Crossing it writes a trigger_event row and opens an opportunity. Nobody clicked anything.

rows.note: Ex-Stripe. Ranks first on raw and nineteenth once access is regressed out. Nothing about the person changed; we stopped paying for the part of the signal that was already priced.

hours_elapsed.basis: wall clock from first_signal_at to asof

sla.badge: First signal 12h ago · decision reached in 38 min · SLA met with 11.8h to spare

rows.note: GitHub was a confirmation source here — first-commit date and cadence, never stars. It did not discover this person and it never discovers anyone.

First-time sole author on a preprint, no company, no domain, no repo, no funding. Twelfth on raw, first once access is regressed out. This is the population the whole design exists to reach, and it is also our thinnest channel — see the error bar on arXiv in the Days of Edge table.

sla.badge: First signal 290h ago · SLA breached by 266h · blocked on: elicitation response (human wait)

rows.note: A solo US operator with no repo, no funding and no network. Fourteenth on raw, second once access is regressed out.

reorder_summary.line: Twenty of twenty rows moved. The reorder is the argument.

live_ingest.label: Ingest now
live_ingest.plain_line: Live-ness here is demonstrated by insertion, not by re-render. This button runs one Hacker News query and appends any new rows to the board, visibly distinct from the precomputed set.
live_ingest.on_failure: Live ingest failed. Nothing was appended. The precomputed board is unaffected.

people._board_people_note: The eighteen records below are the rest of the visible board. They are scored deterministically and carry no cold-start bench, no manifest and no receipt, because LLM calls are hard-capped by a constant to the hero entities — a decision made at hour zero so that the credit budget survives to hour eighteen. The UI labels them rather than letting a judge assume the depth is uniform.

per_ks.axes_disagree_headline: AXES DISAGREE — Founder neutral, Market bear, Idea-vs-Market neutral.
per_ks.note: Ranked first on the raw board and nineteenth once access signals are regressed out. Nothing about the underlying observations changed.

per_nb.axes_disagree_headline: AXES DISAGREE — Founder neutral, Market bear, Idea-vs-Market neutral.

per_ry.note: GitHub appeared here as a confirmation source keyed to the person — first-commit date, commit cadence, artifact existence. It did not discover him and it is never a discovery source.

per_sv.axes_disagree_headline: AXES DISAGREE — Founder neutral, Market bear, Idea-vs-Market neutral.
per_sv.note: Self-filed 1(b) mark, attorney field empty. Three observations, and the interval says so.

per_pk.axes_disagree_headline: AXES DISAGREE — Founder neutral, Market bear, Idea-vs-Market neutral.

per_aj.note: First-time sole author on a preprint. No company, no domain, no repo, no funding, no network. Twelfth on the raw board and first once access signals are regressed out — and with n=2 the interval is 41 points wide, which is the honest way to say we have almost nothing on her yet. Wide is not the same as low.

per_gn.axes_disagree_headline: AXES DISAGREE — Founder bear, Market bear, Idea-vs-Market neutral.

per_ov.axes_disagree_headline: AXES DISAGREE — Founder bear, Market bear, Idea-vs-Market neutral.

per_zr.axes_disagree_headline: AXES DISAGREE — Founder bear, Market bear, Idea-vs-Market neutral.

per_mo.refuter_disabled_reason: The Refuter is disabled on every real-person entity. We build scored dossiers on named private individuals from public records; running an adversarial generator against a real person is not a thing we are willing to demonstrate.
per_mo.provenance_note: Discovered from committed USPTO bulk XML, badged FIXTURE. The filter, the $250 argument and the goods-and-services parse are real; the bulk file is dated and committed rather than pulled live, because the Open Data Portal key needs identity verification a Saturday cannot deliver.

score_definition_strip.plain_line: Three different things get called a founder score. They are not the same and we label which is which.
chain: Founder Score (persistent · belongs to the person)
never averaged with the other two
score_definition_strip.note: The Founder Score is an input to the Founder axis, not a substitute for it, and neither of them is a single number for the opportunity — because there is no single number for the opportunity.

founder_score.plain_line: Two components, both persistent, both append-only. Splitting them is why a credibility problem does not silently erase a build record.
founder_score.never_resets_mechanism: Stored as append-only versions keyed to the person, never to the company. There is no UPDATE and no DELETE anywhere in the worker, so there is no code path that could reset it.

founder.rationale: Four direct observations. The interval narrowed from 37 points to 26 after one elicitation response that produced two externally checkable pointers and one volunteered concession. Improving because the OLS slope band excludes zero, not because the number went up.

market.label: neutral
market.rationale: Six comparables found at this asof, split evenly. Categorical on purpose: there is no number here, so there is nothing that could be averaged with the other two axes even by accident.
market.value_blocked_reason: Market is categorical by construction. A number here would be the first step towards an averaged axis, which this system does not have.

idea_vs_market.rationale: The goods-and-services text on the trademark filing is a machine-readable description of the product, and it maps cleanly onto an observable gap in settlement reconciliation for small marketplaces. The idea survives as stated. Two scored points is not a trend and we say so rather than drawing a line through it.

founder_market_fit.title: Founder–market fit — scored construct with its own interval
founder_market_fit.plain_line: Named in the brief as a soft-skill assessment that should carry a prediction interval. Here is ours, and here is what it is made of.
founder_market_fit.evidence_basis: Lived-domain-exposure atoms extracted from her own written text — named counterparties, named settlement windows, a specific failure mode described in the first person. Never the karma, never the follower count.
founder_market_fit.caveat: Seven atoms is a thin basis and the interval says so. This is a construct we score, not a construct we have calibrated.

cold_start_bench.statement: No track record found. Scoring on 4 proxy signals. Prior weight 71%. Interval [42, 79]. Here is what would narrow it.
cold_start_bench.prior_weight_formula: w = k/(n+k) with k=10 and n=4 direct observations → 10/14 = 0.714. Seventy-one percent of this number is her reference class, not her.
cold_start_bench.interval_note: This is the bench at first scoring. After the elicitation response landed, the interval narrowed to [48.0, 74.0] with the point unchanged — the evidence bought certainty, not a higher score.

reference_class.dimensions: two, declared at hour zero: technical-vs-operator × has-company-domain
reference_class.note: Deliberately two dimensions, not five. A five-dimension class over 63 people gives every cell n=0 or n=1, and a marginal rate wearing a conditional's clothes is worse than an honest marginal rate.

what_would_narrow_it: A Stripe payout export covering the three months claimed — resolves the paying-user count outright.
A dated changelog on the company domain — expected at P=0.38 for this class and currently absent, which is what is widening the interval by 3.2 points.
A second elicitation round on domain experience — the first one moved the width 11 points on three atoms.
Any third-party observable naming her as operator: a partner announcement, a directory listing with a date, an integration page.
what_would_not_narrow_it: A GitHub repository. Not expected for this profile at P=0.11, so its absence is already priced and its presence would tell us little.
A larger follower count on any platform. We do not collect it and would not weight it if we did.

expected_evidence_manifest.title: Expected-evidence manifest
expected_evidence_manifest.plain_line: We wrote down what should exist if the claims were true BEFORE we searched, then went and looked for exactly that.
expected_evidence_manifest.findability_priors_source: Empirical rates over our own 63-person crawl, computed per reference-class cell with the cell count printed. Nothing here is a hardcoded constant.

rows.note: Resolves, serves a real page, TLS certificate issued 2026-05-28.

A live Stripe checkout endpoint. Transacting today. Everyone tracks that a domain was registered; nobody fetches the page, and transacting-vs-parked is the highest-value bit in the whole domain signal.
Three tiers, named, with figures. Feeds the ARPU consistency check on the paying-user claim.
Self-filed, 1(b) intent-to-use, TEAS Plus, attorney field empty. This is the row that opened the file.

Not expected for this profile — found anyway because she named it, and the named thread exists on the stated date. Unexpected presence informs the estimate; unexpected absence would not have cost her anything.
Expected at P=0.38 for an operator with a live company domain and a checkout endpoint. Absent, and a direct fetch returned 404. This widens the interval by 3.2 points. It does not lower the score.
Weakly expected. Absent. Widens by 1.4.
Not expected for a solo non-technical operator with a company domain. P=0.11 from our own crawl. Absence costs her nothing — this is the row that stops the system re-encoding the network gate.
Not expected. She filed a 1(b) intent-to-use mark, which means the product is pre-launch and therefore pre-fundraise by construction. Absence here is the thesis, not a gap.
Not expected. Most early-stage press is a rewritten founder self-report anyway; our reliability table prices it at +0.2 for that reason.

expected_evidence_manifest.closing_line: Four of these ten rows are absences our findability prior already predicted. They are greyed out and they cost her nothing. Missing expected evidence widens the interval and never lowers the score — that asymmetry is the entire cold-start mechanism, expressed as arithmetic rather than as a value statement.

founder_score_history.reason: First scored on the trademark filing and the domain probe. Two observations, so the interval is nearly the whole scale.
Checkout endpoint and pricing page found. Prior weight 71%.
Elicitation response received; named acquisition channel verified against a dated public thread.
Payout receipt fetched. Interval narrowed from 37 points to 26. The point did not move — the evidence bought certainty, not a higher score.

per_mo.milestones_note: The brief says the score sharpens with every milestone. These render as ticks on the history chart; each one is an observation row with a date, not a label someone typed.

per_dr.provenance_note: AUTHORED. This person and both of his ventures were written by us, precisely so that we could plant contradictions and catch them on camera without doing it to a real person. The contradiction fixtures are hosted at real public URLs and the Validator performs a genuine HTTP GET against them.

entity_resolution.title: Two ventures, one person
match_reasons: same code-host handle across both ventures
same registrant email domain on both company domains
entity_resolution.did_not_match_on: name — the spelling differs across the two ventures, which is exactly why we never key the merge on a name
entity_resolution.consequence: The Founder Score carried over. It did not reset when the company changed, because it was never keyed to a company.

score_definition_strip.plain_line: Three different things get called a founder score. They are not the same and we label which is which.
chain: Founder Score (persistent · belongs to the person)
never averaged with the other two
score_definition_strip.note: This is why a contradiction on Ledgerline moves a number that has a 2024 history attached to it. The score follows him. Not this company — him.

founder_score.plain_line: Two components, both persistent, both append-only. The split is why a lie about revenue does not erase a real build record, and why the decision can be conditional rather than a flat pass.
founder_score.never_resets_mechanism: Eight versions across two ventures, all append-only. Version 5 is the row where the venture changes and the value does not.
founder_score.divergence_note: Credibility 44.1 against build capability 64.8 is the whole shape of this deal. He builds; his statements about what he built do not survive a fetch. Averaging those two numbers would destroy exactly the information the investor needs, which is the same reason we never average the three axes.

founder.rationale: Three claims contradicted in four hours on a person with a two-venture history. Declining because the OLS slope band excludes zero at all four asof points, not because we felt the deal turn.

market.label: bear
market.rationale: Eight comparables. The cited comparable raised at Series A, not at this stage, and the sector prior was written down after the last Market verdict in b2b_fintech_infra. Categorical on purpose: there is no number here to average with the other two axes.
market.value_blocked_reason: Market is categorical by construction. Bullish, neutral and bear are not on a scale that could be combined with a founder score.

idea_vs_market.rationale: The idea does not survive scrutiny as stated: the reconciliation wedge is occupied by two better-capitalised comparables. The brief's own definition of this axis asks the second question — is the team strong enough to pivot — and that clause is the one legitimate cross-axis link in the system, so we model it explicitly rather than pretending the axes are independent. Build capability lower bound 55.4 says yes. Stable because the slope band includes zero.
idea_vs_market.reading: Idea fails as-is. This is a pivot bet on the person — and the build-capability lower bound is the axis that says the person can carry it.

per_dr.axes_disagree_headline: AXES DISAGREE — Founder bear, Market bear, Idea-vs-Market neutral. This is a pivot bet on the person. We don't average them and we don't resolve it.
per_dr.axes_disagree_detail: The disagreement that matters is inside the Founder read: credibility is bear at a lower bound of 29.8 while build capability clears the gate at 55.4. Two of three axes point down and the third is neutral, and there is no arithmetic in this system that would turn that into one number. Naming the binding and dissenting axes is the whole output; resolving them would be inventing a view we do not have.

founder_market_fit.title: Founder–market fit — scored construct with its own interval
founder_market_fit.plain_line: Named in the brief as a soft-skill assessment that should carry a prediction interval.
founder_market_fit.evidence_basis: Lived-domain-exposure atoms from written technical answers across both ventures — named settlement counterparties, specific reconciliation failure modes, dated integration details. Scored on atom density, never on how fluent the prose is.
founder_market_fit.caveat: Note this sits well above his credibility score. Knowing the domain and describing your own traction accurately are different things, and collapsing them into one number would hide the only interesting fact about this deal.

per_dr.cold_start_bench_blocked_reason: Not a cold-start founder. Twenty-three direct observations across two ventures, so the prior weight is 10/(23+10) = 30% and the reference class is doing very little of the work. The bench is the wrong instrument here and rendering it would imply a thinness that does not exist.

expected_evidence_manifest.title: Expected-evidence manifest
expected_evidence_manifest.plain_line: What should exist if the deck were true. Written before the search, then searched for exactly.
expected_evidence_manifest.findability_priors_source: Empirical rates over our own 63-person crawl, per reference-class cell, cell count printed.

rows.note: Live, transacting, TLS issued 2026-03-22.
Found — and it is the artifact that contradicts the revenue claim. Presence is not the same as corroboration.

Found. Lists three named people against a claim of twelve. We used a team page and a job board here rather than LinkedIn, because we declined LinkedIn in our own not-collected ledger and it would be incoherent to lean on it for the demo's peak moment.
Found, and dated. Confirmation source only — first-commit date and cadence. Never stars, never followers.
Expected at P=0.58 for a company at the stated revenue. None found, on the company domain or on two job boards. Widens the interval by 2.6 and contributes −1.3 to the revenue claim's log-odds.
Absent but expected at this stage of a diligence conversation. It becomes a gap row in the memo, not a guess.
Absent. Without a post-money we cannot compute implied ownership against the 8–12% thesis target, and the decision card says exactly that rather than showing a number.
Not expected, and not collected either — accelerator rosters are a declined pedigree channel. Grey for two independent reasons.

expected_evidence_manifest.closing_line: Three penalised absences here, widening the interval by 6.7 points in total. None of them lowered the score. What lowered the score was evidence that was found and disagreed.

founder_score_history.reason: First scored. One claim resolved, so the reference class is carrying almost all of it.
Four of four claims verified against third-party observables.
Eight of nine claims verified. This is the record he arrives with.
Venture wound down. No claim resolved, so the score does not move. Winding a company down is not a credibility event and we refuse to treat it as one.
New venture. The score carries over — it belongs to him, not to Kestrel Ops. It drifts down only because seventeen months passed without a resolved claim, which widens rather than punishes.
Founding-date claim contradicted at −2.8. Three contradictions in thirty-eight minutes, against a record of eight verified claims from 2024 that is still sitting in the same line.

apply_form.line: We didn't ask for team size, market or traction.
apply_form.rationale: Everything we did not ask for, we can check. Asking a founder to self-report the numbers we are about to verify is how a diligence process becomes a formality.
apply_form.follow_up_policy: We ask for two fields now. If we need more, we ask one specific question, once, and tell you why.

hours_elapsed.basis: wall clock from first_signal_at to asof

sla.badge: First signal 12h ago · decision reached in 38 min · SLA met with 11.8h to spare

stage_timeline.note: Inbound. The sourcing stage is instantaneous by construction — the applicant sourced themselves. Zero days of edge, which is exactly what the Days of Edge table says about the Apply channel.
Passed all four screen rules: a product artifact exists, in thesis, not an excluded category, company identity present.
Manifest derived, 14 claims extracted, 47 verification fetches issued, three claims contradicted.

deck.provenance_badge: AUTHORED — this deck was written by us, not received from a founder.
deck.authored_reason: We planted four contradictions and one expected-but-absent gap on purpose. Doing that to a real founder's deck would be indefensible; doing it to our own is the only way to show the mechanism working end to end.

hero_slide.bbox_note: Hardcoded for this one fixture deck. Deriving slide coordinates generically earns nothing on camera and costs an afternoon.

claim_distribution.reconciliation: 6 verified + 5 unverified + 3 contradicted = 14 extracted. Plus 2 absent-but-expected added by the manifest = 16.
claim_distribution.plain_line: Trust is scored per claim, never per company. There is no company-level trust average in this system, because averaging a verified incorporation record against a contradicted revenue figure would destroy the only thing an investor needs to see.

claims.claim_text: €41K MRR as of June 2026.
claims.memo_blocked_reason: Contradicted claims do not appear in the memo body. The memo renders from surviving claims only; this one appears in the bear case and nowhere else.

terms.label: Prior — founder self-report, deck slide 7
Corroborating — pricing page tiers are consistent with the stated ARPU
Direct contradiction — changelog cadence and public review volume imply under 200 users
Expected and absent — open roles at the stated revenue (P=0.58, n=21)

log_odds.verdict_sentence: Contradicted. Revenue is overstated by roughly an order of magnitude relative to observable usage. Note that we found one piece of evidence FOR the claim and kept it in the sum — the pricing page is consistent, and removing a corroborating term to make a cleaner story would be the same sin we are catching here.

left.label: Deck slide 7 — cropped region
left.provenance_badge: AUTHORED — this deck was written by us, not received from a founder.
left.caption: The exact pixels the number was read from, with the page and coordinates that produced them. Not a paraphrase of the slide — the slide.

atoms.note: Recurring, monthly and net of churn are three separate assertions folded into one token. None of them is separately evidenced.

right.label: Public changelog — release cadence
right.excerpt: 2026-06-02 · v0.4.1 — retry backoff · 2026-04-30 · v0.4.0 — first public release
right.finding: Two releases in the quarter, first public release 2026-04-30. Six weeks of availability at the stated price implies a customer count two orders of magnitude below what €41K/month requires.
right.verifier_note: A direct GET against a real public URL, with the response body, final URL and fetch timestamp rendered. Not a search index — retrieval, deterministic and cacheable.

right.label: Customer newsroom — review volume
right.excerpt: Trusted by teams at 3 named companies · 11 reviews
right.finding: Eleven reviews and three named customers against a revenue figure requiring roughly 180 paying accounts at the published price.

right.label: Open roles — expected and not found
right.finding: No careers page on the company domain and no listings on two job boards. Expected at P=0.58 (n=21) for a company at the stated revenue. This absence is priced because it was predicted to be unlikely — against a founder with no resources it would have cost nothing.

person_consequence.headline: This follows him. Not this company — him.
person_consequence.detail: The Founder Score that just moved carries a 2024 record from a different company under a different name spelling, where eight of nine claims verified. It was never keyed to Ledgerline, so there was nothing here to reset.

claims.loo_caption: Measured deltas from an exact closed-form recompute with each item removed — not the model narrating its own reasoning. Read the second row: without that one fetched page the claim is merely unverified, not contradicted. One HTTP GET is carrying the entire verdict, and we would rather you knew that than be impressed by the conclusion.

claims.claim_text: 12 employees.
claims.memo_blocked_reason: Contradicted. Appears in the bear case only.

terms.label: Prior — founder self-report, deck slide 4
Direct contradiction — team page lists 3 named people
Direct contradiction — no current or historical job postings on two boards

log_odds.verdict_sentence: Contradicted. Twelve stated against three traceable, which changes the burn and the execution story at the same time. We checked this with a team page and a job board rather than LinkedIn, because we declined LinkedIn in our own not-collected ledger and leaning on it here would make that ledger decorative.

left.label: Deck slide 4 — cropped region
left.provenance_badge: AUTHORED — this deck was written by us, not received from a founder.
left.caption: The slide region the number came from.

right.label: Company team page
right.excerpt: Three named people with roles and photographs.

right.label: Job board history
right.finding: No current or historical postings. Growing to twelve normally leaves a hiring trace somewhere.

claims.loo_caption: Measured deltas from an exact closed-form recompute. Unlike the revenue claim, this verdict survives dropping either single source — two independent artifacts agree.

claims.claim_text: Founded January 2025; 18 months of engineering behind the product.
claims.memo_blocked_reason: Contradicted. Appears in the bear case only.

terms.label: Prior — founder self-report, deck slide 2
Direct contradiction — first observable artifact postdates the stated founding date by 14 months

log_odds.verdict_sentence: Contradicted. The founding date predates the first observable artifact by 14 months, so the stated 18 months of work is not visible anywhere: not in the commit history, not in the certificate transparency log, not in the registry.

left.label: Deck slide 2 — cropped region
left.provenance_badge: AUTHORED — this deck was written by us, not received from a founder.
left.caption: The slide region the date came from.

right.label: Code host — first commit
right.finding: First commit 2026-03-04, fourteen months after the stated founding date. Confirmation source only — a date and a cadence, never a star count.

right.label: Certificate transparency — first TLS issuance
right.finding: The domain did not exist in any usable form before March 2026. Certificate transparency is a public append-only log, which is a pleasing thing to check an append-only ledger against.

person_consequence.headline: Third contradiction. Thirty-eight minutes from first signal.

claims.loo_caption: One evidence item, so one row. Without it the claim is unverified rather than contradicted — a self-report alone is never enough to convict.

claims.claim_text: Comparable company raised at a €60M post in this exact wedge.

terms.label: Prior — founder self-report, deck slide 6
Partial corroboration — the named comparable exists and raised, but at Series A rather than at this stage

log_odds.verdict_sentence: Unverified, not contradicted. The company is real and the round is real; the stage is not the stated stage, which makes the comparison misleading rather than false. This is exactly the kind of claim that a company-level trust average would erase.

evidence.verifier_note: Tavily is the right tool here and only here: this is a claim about an entity that already exists on the public web, which is what a search index is for. The receipt panes on the contradicted claims use direct fetches instead, because an index cannot be expected to have crawled a page we are checking today.

claims.claim_text: Ledgerline GmbH is incorporated and in good standing.

terms.label: Prior — founder self-report
Registry filing — commercial register entry, dated
Corroborating — imprint page names the same entity and registration number

log_odds.verdict_sentence: Verified. Registry filings are the strongest row in our reliability table because perjury risk attaches to them.

claims.claim_text: The product is live and accepting payment.

terms.label: Prior — founder self-report
Live checkout endpoint responds
Pricing page with named tiers and figures
Dated changelog with release tags

log_odds.verdict_sentence: Verified. He built a thing and it takes money. Note that the same changelog which verifies this claim is the artifact that contradicts the revenue claim — evidence is scored per claim, and a source is not a friend or an enemy.

claims.claim_text: The Ledgerline mark is filed.

terms.label: Prior — founder self-report
Registry filing — application on file with a serial number and date
Corroborating — goods-and-services text matches the described product

log_odds.verdict_sentence: Verified. Note this filing has an attorney of record, unlike the outbound hero's — which is precisely the access signal we regress out of the board ranking.

evidence.excerpt: serial 98/441,207 · filed 2026-04-08 · attorney of record present

claims.claim_text: This person previously operated Kestrel Ops (2024–2025).
claims.derived_note: Not asserted by the founder. The deck does not mention the prior venture at all. This claim was derived by entity resolution on a shared code-host handle and a shared registrant email domain — never on the name, whose spelling differs across the two ventures.

terms.label: Prior — no self-report exists; this claim was derived, not asserted
Registry filing — 2024 incorporation with the same registrant email domain
Corroborating — archived team page under the same code-host handle

log_odds.verdict_sentence: Verified, and the strongest claim in the file. The prior venture is where eight of nine claims verified, which is the record his score arrives carrying and the reason the decision is conditional rather than a flat pass.

claims.claim_text: Operates in settlement reconciliation for B2B fintech infrastructure.

terms.label: Prior — founder self-report
Goods-and-services text on the mark describes settlement reconciliation
Pricing page describes per-settlement-run billing
Changelog entries reference reconciliation primitives

log_odds.verdict_sentence: Verified, and it clears the thesis hard filter on sector.

evidence.excerpt: Software for reconciling settlement records between payment processors and merchant ledgers.
evidence.verifier_note: The goods-and-services free text is a machine-readable statement of what the product is. We embed it and feed it straight into the Idea-vs-Market axis.

claims.claim_text: There is a real codebase with sustained commit activity.

terms.label: Prior — founder self-report
Code host — first commit date, confirmation source only
Code host — commit cadence sustained over 19 weeks
Release tags visible in the public changelog
Live endpoint serving the built artifact

log_odds.verdict_sentence: Verified. This is the claim behind the build-capability lower bound of 55.4, which is the only reason this deal is conditional and not a pass. GitHub is used here purely as confirmation — first-commit date, cadence, artifact existence. Never stars, never followers, and never as a way of finding anyone.

claims.claim_text: €180K of qualified pipeline.

terms.label: Prior — founder self-report
Weak corroboration — a trade press mention names two prospective customers

log_odds.verdict_sentence: Unverified. Pipeline is unobservable from outside by construction — there is no artifact a healthy pipeline would necessarily produce, so absence here is not evidence and the manifest did not predict one. Unverified is the honest resting state, not a soft accusation.

claims.claim_text: 14 design partners.

terms.label: Prior — founder self-report
Corroborating — customers page names three companies with logos

log_odds.verdict_sentence: Unverified, and confidence is Low because the sum sits within half a point of zero. Three named against fourteen claimed is a gap, but a design partner has no obligation to be listed publicly, so this is genuinely undetermined rather than quietly damning.

evidence.excerpt: Trusted by teams at 3 named companies

claims.claim_text: Under 2% monthly logo churn.

terms.label: Prior — founder self-report, and nothing else exists

log_odds.verdict_sentence: Unverified with zero evidence either way. The manifest predicted no observable artifact for churn at this company size, so its absence is not penalised and the claim simply sits at the self-report prior. A system that punished this would be punishing the founder for our inability to check.

claims.claim_text: LTV:CAC of 4.1x.

terms.label: Prior — founder self-report
Expected and absent — a stated acquisition channel with observable spend (P=0.31, n=19)

log_odds.verdict_sentence: Unverified. A ratio derived from two numbers we could not observe individually is not a third fact, and we decline to treat it as one.

claims.claim_text: Cap table — not disclosed.
claims.posterior_blocked_reason: No claim was asserted, so there is nothing to score. This row exists because the manifest predicted the artifact at P=0.44 and it was absent. A gap is priced as a wider interval, never as a lower score and never as an invented number.

terms.label: Expected and absent — cap table disclosure (P=0.44, n=19)

log_odds.arithmetic_string: no assertion to score — the effect is on the interval, not on a posterior
log_odds.verdict_sentence: Absent but expected. Widens the interval by 1.9 points. The memo prints 'Cap table: not disclosed' and the renderer physically cannot substitute a number for it.

claims.claim_text: Post-money valuation and instrument — not disclosed.
claims.posterior_blocked_reason: No claim was asserted. Without a post-money figure the implied ownership against the 8–12% thesis target cannot be computed, and the decision card says exactly that instead of showing a percentage.

terms.label: Expected and absent — round terms disclosure (P=0.49, n=19)

log_odds.arithmetic_string: no assertion to score — the effect is on the interval, not on a posterior
log_odds.verdict_sentence: Absent but expected. This is the single most likely place a fabricated number could reach the screen in a product like this, which is exactly why it renders as a refusal with the thesis target printed beside it.

memo.title: Investment memo — Ledgerline
memo.padding_note: Five sections plus a gaps block and a bear case. The brief's appendix lists thirteen candidate sections; we ship the five it requires. A short memo that marks its own holes reads as more trustworthy than a long one that fills them invisibly, and padding counts against us.
memo.rule: Every prose bullet is templated from claims that survived verification, and every bullet carries at least one evidence_id. A bullet with zero evidence_ids physically cannot render as prose — it renders blocked, with its reason. Fabricated financials are a schema violation here, not a prompt failure.
memo.hypotheses_exception: Investment Hypotheses is the one section permitted LLM-authored prose, because it requires synthesis rather than extraction and a list of facts is not an argument. The constraint is unchanged: every bullet carries at least one evidence_id or it is blocked. One of ours is blocked, and we left it visible.
memo.plain_line: Everything below survived a check. The things that did not survive are in the bear case, and the things we could not establish are in the gaps block.

bullets.text: Ledgerline GmbH, incorporated 2026-03-22, registration HRB 284119, settlement reconciliation between payment processors and merchant ledgers.
The product is live and accepting payment: a responding checkout endpoint, a pricing page with three named tiers, and a dated changelog with release tags from 2026-04-30.
Sector b2b_fintech_infra, stage seed, region DACH — clears all three thesis hard filters.
Market size: no citable source found. A bottom-up estimate from n=2 comparables would carry low confidence and we decline to print it as a figure.
bullets.note: Rendered as a pointer into the gaps block rather than as a blocked bullet, because the absence is the finding.

sections.title: Investment hypotheses
sections.llm_authored_note: Synthesis, not extraction — but every bullet still carries evidence_ids or it does not render.

bullets.text: The thesis here is the operator, not the company. A verified 2024 record of eight claims resolving out of nine, against a live codebase with nineteen consecutive weeks of commits, describes someone who ships. The build-capability lower bound of 55.4 is the only number in this file that clears the conviction gate, and it is the reason this is a conversation rather than a pass.

The wedge is real and currently occupied. Settlement reconciliation for mid-market processors is a genuine operational gap — the goods-and-services text describes it precisely and the changelog shows it being built — but two better-capitalised comparables are already in it, which is what drives the Market axis to bear and the Idea-vs-Market read to requires_pivot.

The stated traction is the risk, and it is a disclosure risk rather than an execution risk. Revenue, headcount and founding date each failed a fetch inside forty minutes. Nothing we found suggests he cannot build; everything we found suggests his description of what he has built does not survive contact with the public record.

bullets.reason: no evidence_id — bullet blocked from rendering

strengths.text: A two-venture operating history on one person, with eight of nine claims verified in the prior venture.
A shipped, transacting product with nineteen consecutive weeks of commit activity behind it.

weaknesses.text: Three material claims contradicted by direct fetch within thirty-eight minutes of first signal.
Three employees traceable against twelve stated, which changes the burn model and the execution story at once.

opportunities.text: The reconciliation primitive shipping in the changelog is narrower and more defensible than the positioning in the deck, and a pivot into it is a smaller move than the deck implies.

threats.text: The named comparable raised at Series A in the same wedge, so the capital gap is structural rather than temporary.

bullets.text: Problem: settlement records from payment processors and internal merchant ledgers drift apart, and reconciling them is manual at mid-market scale. This is stated in the trademark's goods-and-services text, which is a machine-readable description of the product filed under penalty of a registry process.
Product: a hosted reconciliation service billed per settlement run, at €89, €229 and €640 per month across three tiers, live since the first public release on 2026-04-30.
Evidence it is real rather than announced: the checkout endpoint responds, and the release tags are dated and sequential.

sections.title: Traction & KPIs
sections.plain_line: This section is deliberately short. The revenue and headcount figures were contradicted, so they are not here — they are in the bear case. Contradictions reach the investor before the memo does.

bullets.text: Three named customers with logos on a public page, against fourteen design partners stated. Unverified rather than contradicted: a design partner has no obligation to be listed publicly.
Eleven public reviews and two releases in the quarter. This is the observable usage surface, and it is the measurement the revenue claim failed against.

€180K stated qualified pipeline and 4.1x stated LTV:CAC both remain unverified with no observable artifact predicted for either. Printed as stated figures with their state attached, never as findings.

Monthly logo churn under 2%: stated, zero evidence either way, and the manifest predicted no observable artifact at this company size. It stays at the self-report prior rather than being penalised for our inability to check it.
bullets.render_note: Permitted without an evidence_id because it renders the claim's STATE, not the claim's content as fact. The number appears in quotation marks attributed to the founder, never as a KPI.

gaps_block.title: What we could not establish
gaps_block.plain_line: The renderer physically cannot make these up.

rows.label: Cap table
rows.basis: a refusal, not a measurement — there is no sample size behind 'we do not know this'

rows.label: Post-money valuation
rows.value: not disclosed — ownership cannot be computed against the 8–12% thesis target

rows.label: Open roles
rows.value: expected at P=0.58 (n=21) for a company at the stated revenue; none found

rows.label: Market size
rows.value: no citable source found — bottom-up from n=2 comparables, low confidence

bullets.text: Revenue is overstated by roughly an order of magnitude relative to observable usage.
Team is overstated 12 against 3 traceable, which changes the burn and the execution story simultaneously.
Founding date predates the first observable artifact by 14 months, so the stated 18 months of work is not visible anywhere.
Dissenting axis: Market is bear at n=8 and declining, on comparables that raised at later stages. Nothing in the Founder read offsets it, because we do not offset axes.

portfolio_conflict_check.text: No conflicting position in thesis sector (checked against 4 seeded positions).

decision.ownership_blocked_reason: Cannot compute — post-money not disclosed (thesis target 8–12%).
decision.ownership_render: Ownership: cannot compute — post-money not disclosed (thesis target 8–12%).
decision.binding_axis_reason: Founder axis 44.1 [29.8, 58.4], declining, n=23. Three claims contradicted in four hours on a person with a two-venture history.
decision.dissenting_axis_reason: Market is BEAR at n=8. Nothing in the founder read offsets a bear market read, because we do not offset axes.
decision.axes_disagree_headline: AXES DISAGREE — Founder bear, Market bear, Idea-vs-Market neutral. This is a pivot bet on the person. We don't average them and we don't resolve it.
decision.gate_rule_applied: ladder step 3 — width inside appetite, founder LCB below threshold, build-capability LCB clears it
decision.gate_sentence: Width 28.6 is inside the appetite of 30 at risk_appetite=medium. The founder-credibility lower bound of 29.8 is far below the 55 gate — but the build-capability lower bound of 55.4 clears it, which is what makes this conditional and not a pass. At risk_appetite=low (max width 20) this becomes probe_further.

conditions_to_close.text: Stripe payout export covering the three months claimed.
Written headcount with contract dates for all named team members.

falsification_conditions_before_wire.text: Stripe export shows under €8K MRR — this is a pass, not a renegotiation.
Any named team member is not under contract.

decision.falsification_naming_note: Named 'falsification conditions before wire', not 'kill criteria' — kill criteria are post-investment monitoring, which is out of scope.

next_action.text: Send the three-condition list and hold the position.

decision.portfolio_conflict: No conflicting position in thesis sector.

sourcing_origin.title: Self-filed trademark application — 1(b) intent-to-use, no attorney
sourcing_origin.goods_and_services_text: Software as a service featuring software for reconciling settlement and payout records for online marketplace sellers.
sourcing_origin.argument: Two hundred and fifty dollars against a patent's ten thousand. A self-filed mark with an empty attorney field does four jobs at once: someone is building, they have no law firm, therefore no funding, therefore no network. It is typically filed at or before domain registration. Every vendor in this market mines patents; nobody mines this. And the goods-and-services free text is a machine-readable description of the product, so it feeds the Idea-vs-Market axis directly rather than being scraped prose.
sourcing_origin.provenance_note: Parsed from committed USPTO bulk XML — seven days, downloaded and committed at hour one. The filter, the $250 argument and the goods-and-services parse are all real; the file is dated rather than pulled live because the Open Data Portal key requires identity verification a Saturday cannot reliably deliver.
sourcing_origin.known_coverage_limit: Foreign-domiciled applicants have been required to appoint US counsel since 2019, so the empty-attorney marker selects for US-domiciled founders. Our outbound hero is therefore a US-domiciled solo operator, and we name the limitation rather than let you find it.
sourcing_origin.post_filter_gate: Pass condition written before parsing: at least 25 post-filter rows and at least 1 with a live checkout endpoint. 31 rows, 4 transacting. Passed.

domain_probe.title: Domain page probe — transacting, not merely registered
domain_probe.argument: Everyone tracks that a domain was registered. Nobody fetches the page. Transacting-versus-parked is the highest-value bit in the entire domain signal and every vendor discards it. She is transacting today.

sla.badge: First signal 290h ago · SLA breached by 266h · blocked on: elicitation response (human wait)

stage_timeline.note: The signal existed for 11 days before the conviction threshold was crossed. That lag is the channel's days-of-edge, not our latency.

opp_northgate.elapsed_blocked_reason: No decision reached. The clock is still running and the badge says so rather than reporting a median we have not earned.

elicitation.title: Elicitation — we generate the evidence rather than retrieving it
elicitation.plain_line: The interval was too wide to commit $100K. Rather than guess, the system worked out which single question would narrow it most, and asked exactly that one.
elicitation.selection_method: value of information — expected reduction in posterior interval width, computed across all 9 open claims
elicitation.challenge_text: You say 40 paying users. Name the channel that produced the first ten, and paste one Stripe payout date we can verify.
elicitation.challenge_design: Falsifiable, and it demands a checkable pointer rather than a story. Not 'tell us about traction'. Fluent, atom-free prose scores near zero here; only someone who did the work produces atoms that corroborate, which is why a polished ex-FAANG founder cannot beat this by pedigree.

outreach.not_sent_statement: Drafted and rendered. Never sent. Outbound messages to real private individuals discovered from public records are not something we will demonstrate by actually sending them.
outreach.draft_body: I came across your trademark filing from 2026-07-07 (serial 98/447,913) and the live checkout on northgatesettle.com. Before I form a view I'd rather ask than assume: you say 40 paying users — which channel produced the first ten, and is there a Stripe payout date I can check?

response.provenance_badge: AUTHORED — this response was written by us, not received from a founder.
response.authored_statement: We correctly never sent the message, so no real reply exists. This response is authored by us and badged as such, and we say it out loud rather than letting the honesty frame break at the one moment it matters most.
response.source_class_note: Typed as an interview excerpt in the ledger. The brief names interviews as a Memory ingest type and asks for interview-excerpt citations; this is one row serving both.

checkable_atom_density.basis: named entities, dates and figures per 100 words, over a 44-word response

concession.basis: volunteered a specific weakness — 40 is signups, 11 are paying

scoring.concession_note: Conceding scores up. A founder who volunteers the weaker number before we find it is telling us something no polished answer could.
scoring.magnitude_disclaimer: The magnitude of the interval contraction is a hand-set prior with no calibration set behind it. The mechanism is what we are claiming, not the size of the move — and this is the single most attackable number in our pitch, so we say it before you ask.

claims.claim_text: 40 paying users.

terms.label: Prior — founder self-report
Interview excerpt — 2 of 3 offered pointers verified, one concession volunteered
Corroborating — Reddit thread named as the acquisition channel, thread found and dated
Expected and absent — dated changelog (P=0.38, n=21)

log_odds.verdict_sentence: Still unverified, not verified. It moved from −1.2 to +0.7 on evidence that did not exist forty minutes ago — and she volunteered that 40 is signups and 11 are paying, which is why the count itself is not the corroborated fact.

left.label: Interview excerpt — elicitation response
left.excerpt: First ten came from the r/smallbusiness weekly thread on 2026-06-28, not from ads. Stripe payout 2026-07-16, po_1PxK…, $412. I'll be straight with you: 40 is signups, 11 are paying. The rest are on a free tier I haven't shut off yet.
left.provenance_badge: AUTHORED — this response was written by us, not received from a founder.
left.caption: Typed as an interview excerpt in the ledger. The brief names interviews as a Memory ingest type and asks for interview-excerpt citations; this is one row serving both.

atoms.note: self-report, unverifiable externally — logged as a concession, not as traction

right.label: Named acquisition channel
right.finding: The named thread exists on the stated date.

right.label: Stripe payout receipt
right.finding: Payout id format valid; amount consistent with 11 paying at the stated price.

person_consequence.headline: Interval narrowed from 37 points to 26.
person_consequence.note: The point did not move. The evidence bought certainty, not a higher score — which is what a narrowing interval is supposed to look like.

claims.loo_caption: Measured deltas from an exact closed-form recompute with each item removed.

claims.claim_text: The product is live and accepting payment.
claims.derived_note: Derived from the domain probe, not asserted by anyone. There was no deck and no application — this claim exists because we fetched the page.

terms.label: Prior — no self-report; derived from a fetch
Live Stripe checkout endpoint responds
Pricing page with named tiers
Payout receipt consistent with the published price

log_odds.verdict_sentence: Verified. She is transacting today. No database in this market carries this fact, because carrying it requires fetching the page rather than reading the registration.

claims.claim_text: A self-filed 1(b) trademark application exists with no attorney of record.

terms.label: Registry filing — USPTO application record, dated, with an empty attorney field

log_odds.verdict_sentence: Verified on a single term, because a registry filing is the strongest row in our reliability table and there was no self-report to discount. This is the row that opened the file.

evidence.excerpt: 1(b) intent-to-use · TEAS Plus · attorney: (empty) · owner: individual

claims.claim_text: A company domain exists and serves a real page.

terms.label: Live page served, TLS certificate issued 2026-05-28
Certificate transparency log confirms first issuance date

log_odds.verdict_sentence: Verified.

claims.claim_text: The first ten users came from a named community thread rather than paid acquisition.

terms.label: Prior — founder self-report
Interview excerpt — named a specific dated thread rather than a channel category
Corroborating — the named thread exists on the stated date

log_odds.verdict_sentence: Unverified but moving. The thread exists on the date she gave; that the first ten users came from it is not externally observable and we do not pretend otherwise.

claims.claim_text: Dated changelog — expected and absent.
claims.posterior_blocked_reason: No claim was asserted. The manifest predicted a dated changelog at P=0.38 for this reference class and a direct fetch returned 404. This widens the interval by 3.2 points and does not lower the score.

terms.label: Expected and absent — dated changelog (P=0.38, n=21)

log_odds.arithmetic_string: −0.4 = −0.4 (applied to the users claim; no posterior of its own)
log_odds.verdict_sentence: Absent but expected. The single most narrowing thing she could publish.

claims.claim_text: Open roles posting — weakly expected and absent.
claims.posterior_blocked_reason: No claim was asserted. Expected only weakly at P=0.29 for a solo operator pre-launch, so the penalty is correspondingly small — 1.4 points of interval, no change to the score.

terms.label: Expected and absent — open roles posting (P=0.29, n=21)

log_odds.verdict_sentence: Absent but expected, weakly.

claims.claim_text: Cap table — not applicable at this stage.
claims.posterior_blocked_reason: Not disclosed, and not expected to be — she filed a 1(b) intent-to-use mark, which means pre-launch and therefore pre-fundraise by construction. Logged as a gap so that the memo route has something honest to render, not as a penalty.

terms.label: Not expected for this profile — not penalised

log_odds.arithmetic_string: 0.0 — absence predicted by the findability prior, so it costs nothing
log_odds.verdict_sentence: Absent, expected to be absent, not penalised. This is the grey row.

claims.claim_text: Customer references — unavailable at this stage.
claims.posterior_blocked_reason: Eleven paying users on a free-tier-heavy product does not produce citable references yet. Absent, expected to be absent, not penalised.

terms.label: Not expected for this profile — not penalised

log_odds.arithmetic_string: 0.0 — absence predicted by the findability prior, so it costs nothing
log_odds.verdict_sentence: Absent, expected to be absent, not penalised.

opp_northgate.claims_note: Outbound: no deck. Claims are derived from observations and from the elicitation response, and follow the identical claim shape. Nine claims against Ledgerline's sixteen, because there was no deck to extract from — which is the point: the file was opened by a $250 filing and a page fetch, not by an application.
opp_northgate.memo_blocked_reason: Not yet at decision stage. Blocked on elicitation follow-up. The memo route renders the empty state, not a placeholder memo.

decision.ownership_blocked_reason: No round in market — pre-fundraise by construction (1(b) intent-to-use filing).
decision.ownership_render: Ownership: not applicable — no round in market.
decision.gate_rule_applied: ladder step 4 — width passes, LCB below threshold, n=4 < 8
decision.gate_sentence: After the elicitation response the interval narrowed from 37 to 26 and now clears the medium-risk width bar; before the response it did not. The lower bound of 48 is still 7 points under the 55 gate, and with n=4 direct observations thin evidence does not convert into a rejection. At risk_appetite=high this becomes conditional.

next_action.text: Second elicitation round on domain experience.

decision.portfolio_conflict: No conflicting position in thesis sector.

honesty.plain_line: Everything on this page is a limitation we found in our own system and chose to show you.
honesty.closing_line: Here is what we could not validate in twenty-one hours, and here is the experiment that would.

days_of_edge.title: Days of Edge — median lag from channel signal to consensus visibility
days_of_edge.plain_language: How many days earlier this channel finds someone than the market does.
days_of_edge.method: One pass over observation.observed_at at this asof. Consensus visibility = the first observation about that person whose source is a press mention, a funding announcement, or a startup-database entry. First signal to that date, in days. Pure date arithmetic — no outcome labels, no model recognition, nothing to leak. Intervals are distribution-free order-statistic intervals on the median; below n=6 the bar is the observed range and is labelled as such. People the market has not noticed yet are censored, counted, and excluded from the median rather than silently scored as zero.
days_of_edge.design_rule: A channel qualifies as discovery only if it can fire for a person with no GitHub, no funding and no network.
days_of_edge.render_hint: horizontal bar chart with asymmetric error bars; n printed inside every bar; declined channels greyed; rows with basis='insufficient_data' render the statement instead of a bar

rows.statement: n=1. This is a single observed lag, not a median, and it has no error bar worth the name. It is printed so the channel is visible, and it should not be quoted as a measurement until n grows. 32 further person(s) from this channel are still invisible to the market and are excluded from the median rather than scored as zero — dropping them silently would bias every channel downward.
rows.recommendation: UNDEREXPLORED — recommend investing here. Edge 319d at n=1, but only 1 observation(s) in the last 30 days. High edge, low volume, cold-start-native. PROVISIONAL at n=1: too thin to be a measurement. The recommendation here is to COLLECT MORE, which is the cheap half of acting on it anyway.
rows.limitation: Foreign-domiciled applicants have been required to appoint US counsel since 2019, so the empty-attorney marker selects for US-domiciled founders. We name this rather than let you find it.
rows.note: n=1. This is a single observed lag, not a median, and it has no error bar worth the name. It is printed so the channel is visible, and it should not be quoted as a measurement until n grows. 32 further person(s) from this channel are still invisible to the market and are excluded from the median rather than scored as zero — dropping them silently would bias every channel downward.

channel_outcomes.title: Channel quality by funded outcome
channel_outcomes.display: 0 funded outcomes. This is why we do not rank channels on quality yet — here is the schema that would.

sourcing_graph.title: The sourcing graph — how founders become visible
sourcing_graph.plain_line: Not a list of channels. A graph of the programs, institutions and individuals a founder passes through before anyone in this market notices them.

nodes.label: Public registries (USPTO, commercial register, certificate transparency)
Accelerator cohorts
nodes.declined_reason: Admission IS the network gate. Sourcing from it would rebuild exactly what we are trying to route around.

nodes.label: Investor-to-investor introduction
nodes.declined_reason: The canonical network gate. Not collected, not modelled as a source, and named here so its absence is a decision rather than an oversight.

sourcing_graph.finding: Every node we source from is one a person can reach without knowing anyone. Every node we declined is one where an introduction is the entry condition. That is the whole design rule, drawn as a graph.

rows.reason_text: OAuth required. Not obtainable inside the sprint.
Registration approval latency exceeds the sprint.

rows.source_name: Any JS-rendered source requiring a headless browser
rows.reason_text: Playwright is too fragile at 3am. Any source needing JS rendering is cut, not debugged.
The brief names hackathons. Declined: Cloudflare, JS-rendered, partly non-public.

rows.source_name: GitHub Trending as a discovery surface
rows.reason_text: Ranking repos by stars is track-record sourcing with extra steps. It measures who is already visible — zero days of edge on our own metric, see the table above — and it structurally cannot fire for a founder with no GitHub, which is the exact founder this project is built for. GitHub is welcome as a CONFIRMATION source keyed to a person we already found (first-commit date, commit cadence, artifact existence), never as discovery.
Measures who is already visible. Zero days of edge on our own metric.
They measure the already-visible. Zero expected edge.
A research project, not a collector. arXiv covers the 'papers' ingest instead.

Encodes access, not capability. Ranking on it rebuilds the network gate this product exists to replace. Also ToS. This is why we checked the Ledgerline headcount claim with a team page and a job board rather than LinkedIn.
Named in the brief as a Memory ingest. Declined: it measures audience, not capability, and it is the single most pedigree-loaded signal available.
Cloudflare and ToS. Retained in the pitch as hand-authored hero evidence, labelled 'illustrated, not crawled'.

recognition_probe.title: Recognition probe — model leakage on pre-fame artifacts
recognition_probe.plain_language: Before claiming a hit rate, we tested whether the model already knows the answer. It does.
recognition_probe.method: Fifteen real 2021 pre-fame artifacts, redacted and paraphrased, shown to the model with one question: name the company.
recognition_probe.statement: We wanted to backtest against real outcomes. We ran the leakage probe first; the model identified nine of fifteen redacted artifacts. So we are not reporting a hit rate, because it would be memory, not skill. Here is the probe.

examples.redacted_excerpt: a two-sided marketplace for short-term room rental in one city
a batch-processing library for columnar data on a single node
2021 trademark filing, devtools, sole individual owner

reliability_table.title: Source reliability — published in advance, defended line by line
reliability_table.plain_line: These weights are ours. We set them by hand, we print them, and we will argue any row. Nothing here was learned and nothing here came out of a language model.

rows.rationale: A founder asserting their own metric is the weakest evidence in the system. Negative, not zero.
Neutral prior. Interview and elicitation content is scored on atom density and verified-pointer yield, never on the fact that it was said.
Neutral. We score the text for lived domain exposure, never the karma.
Barely positive. Most early-stage press is a rewritten founder self-report.
First-commit dates and cadence only. Confirmation, never discovery.
A live endpoint, a dated changelog, a team page. Costly to fake, cheap to check.
USPTO and incorporation records. Perjury risk attaches. Strongest row in the table.

confidence_mapping.note: High/Medium/Low is the default render for a non-technical reader. The log-odds number is one click down, never the first thing shown.

research_area_3_design.title: How we would test whether public footprints predict founder success
research_area_3_design.note: Design only. Not run. The brief asks us to document the approach.
research_area_3_design.outcome_variable: Reaching a priced institutional round within 24 months of first observable artifact. Binary, dated and publicly checkable — chosen because it leaves a trace, unlike 'success'.
research_area_3_design.cohort_construction: Matched contemporaneous cohort built from artifact registries rather than funding databases, because sampling on funding conditions on the outcome. For each positive, k=3 controls matched on sector, region, artifact type and artifact date (±30d), drawn from the same channel crawl so selection into the frame is identical.
research_area_3_design.selection_diagnosis: Failure leaves no public trace, so the negative class is systematically under-observed and the sample is censored on the dependent variable. This is a Heckman-type selection problem. The correct estimator is inverse-probability weighting on P(observable | reference class), with the findability priors we already compute serving as the first stage, and contactability (63 discovered → 21 contactable) as the instrument. We diagnose it; we did not code it.
research_area_3_design.leakage_control: The recognition probe above. A leak rate of 0.60 (n=15) on 2021 artifacts means any LLM-derived feature over a pre-2023 cohort is contaminated. Controls: date-restricted retrieval, paraphrase-and-redact, dropping every artifact the model can identify, and a held-out post-cutoff cohort.
research_area_3_design.power_sketch: Detecting an odds ratio of 2.0 at 80% power, alpha 0.05, with a 12% base rate needs roughly 380 positives and 1,140 matched controls. We have 63 people and zero outcomes. That gap is the honest answer.

n_words.basis: word count of the five component fields

latency.title: Compute latency — unattended batch run
latency.render_gate: This tile renders only when logs/batch_7f3a91.jsonl exists on disk. worker/export_demo.py sets log_present after the unattended batch run. Until then these rows are the expected SHAPE of the measurement, not the measurement — and the panel stays hidden rather than showing numbers we have not earned.
latency.note: This tile reads from the real batch log or it does not appear. It measures COMPUTE, not time-to-decision — those live in funnel.timing and are four orders of magnitude apart.

cache_hit_rate.basis: content-hash disk cache over 197 external calls

constraints_on_screen: Real people are pseudonymized: initials + channel + signals only.
The Refuter is disabled on every real-person entity.
Outbound messages are drafted and rendered. None was sent.
The elicitation response is authored by us and badged AUTHORED. We could not send the message, so no real reply exists.
The trademark channel runs on committed bulk XML, badged FIXTURE. The filter, the $250 argument and the goods-and-services parse are real.
There is no averaged axis anywhere in this system. Not in the schema, not in this file, not on screen.

what_we_could_not_validate.title: What we could not validate in twenty-one hours, and the experiment that would

rows.item: Whether the elicitation interval contraction is the right magnitude
rows.why: The update magnitude is a hand-set prior with no calibration set.
rows.experiment: Collect 200 elicitation responses with later-resolved claims; fit the update from realized resolution rates.

rows.item: Whether findability priors generalize beyond our 63-person crawl
rows.why: Several cells are under n=8 and are shrunk to the margin.
rows.experiment: Expand the crawl to 5,000 people across four sectors; report per-cell coverage.

rows.item: Whether Days of Edge predicts anything about outcome quality
rows.why: Zero funded outcomes. channel_outcome is empty by design.
rows.experiment: The empty table is the experiment. It fills over 18 months.

rows.item: Whether the access-neutralized ranking is better, not just different
rows.why: No outcome labels, and the recognition probe says LLM-derived labels would be memory.

asof_slices.note: The point-in-time control. Selecting a slice swaps the scored blocks wholesale. Identical code path — asof=now is the live brain, asof=past is a backtest.

ui_rules.default_state: Every panel's default state is the plain-language version with the quant collapsed. Never the reverse.
ui_rules.n_badge_rule: Every number rendered on screen displays its n. An object with a `value` and no sibling `n` renders as an error, not as a number. `n: null` is legal ONLY when accompanied by a `basis` string.
ui_rules.confidence_render: Confidence renders as High / Medium / Low by default. The log-odds number appears on click.
ui_rules.provenance_filter: The ledger view supports filtering to provenance_class='live' only, so a judge can tell real from authored instantly.
ui_rules.refusal_render_rule: A null with a sibling *_blocked_reason or *_render string MUST render the reason string. Rendering the refusal is the feature. Never hide the field, never substitute a number.
ui_rules.empty_state_text: No data at this asof. Move the point-in-time control forward.
ui_rules.error_state_text: This panel could not render. The rest of the page is unaffected.
ui_rules.live_ingest_failure_text: Live ingest failed. Nothing was appended. The precomputed board is unaffected.
